skill.name: database-architect
skill.description: Design database architectures, schemas, indexes, migrations, and scaling plans for SQL, NoSQL, time-series, graph, and cloud data systems.
skill.summary: Plan scalable database technology, schema, migration, and operational architecture.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported C2, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance patterns, but review found prose-only database architecture guidance in SKILL.md. No executable code, external network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: C2 Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The static C2 finding at SKILL.md line 155 is a false positive from database compliance terminology. The line describes compliance architecture and does not contain command-and-control behavior, executable code, or external communication instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context is plain Markdown guidance about compliance patterns. There is no code path, network endpoint, or instruction to contact a controller.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are false positives in descriptive database architecture text. SKILL.md contains no hashing implementation, encryption implementation, cryptographic API use, or recommendation to use weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown skill description with architecture topics and examples. It contains no cryptographic code or instructions to use insecure algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance findings are false positives from architecture vocabulary such as monitoring, profiling, cloud design, and constraints. The skill does not instruct agents to enumerate hosts, scan networks, collect system secrets, or probe services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations describe database design, diagrams, and operational planning. I found no commands, scanning workflow, or data collection instruction.

content.value_statement: Database choices made too early can create performance, migration, and scaling problems. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design data models, storage choices, and rollout plans with clear trade-offs.
actual_capabilities: Recommends SQL, NoSQL, time-series, graph, search, and cloud database technologies based on access patterns.
Designs conceptual, logical, and physical schemas with relationships, constraints, indexes, and partitioning choices.
Plans migration strategies, rollback paths, backup needs, and staged rollout approaches.
Explains scaling options such as replication, sharding, caching, multi-region design, and workload isolation.
Covers security and compliance topics such as encryption, access control, retention, masking, and audit logging.
Can produce ERD guidance and Mermaid diagram plans when the user requests diagrams.
limitations: It provides architecture recommendations and does not directly inspect a live database unless the host tool supplies data.
It cannot guarantee performance without real workload metrics, query plans, and production constraints.
It may need application, traffic, compliance, and team operations details before making a final technology choice.
It should not execute production migrations or destructive schema changes without explicit approval and tested rollback plans.

use_cases.title: Plan a new product data layer
use_cases.description: Choose the database model, schema boundaries, indexes, and growth strategy before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Redesign a strained legacy database
use_cases.description: Map current pain points to normalization, denormalization, partitioning, caching, or migration options.

use_cases.title: Prepare a cloud database migration
use_cases.description: Compare managed database services and outline rollout, validation, backup, and rollback steps.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a database
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a database for a new application. Ask me for the domain, read and write patterns, data size, consistency needs, team skills, and budget. Then recommend one primary option and two alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Design a schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a database schema for this product: [describe product]. Include main entities, relationships, constraints, indexes, normalization choices, and where denormalization might help.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration plan from [current database] to [target database]. Include phases, data validation, rollback strategy, downtime risk, observability, and cutover criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Architect for scale
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a database architecture for [system] with [traffic], [data volume], [regions], and [consistency requirements]. Cover sharding, replication, caching, indexing, failure modes, and operational trade-offs.

output_examples.input: Design a data layer for a multi-tenant SaaS billing platform.
output: A recommended relational core for transactional billing records.
Tenant isolation options with trade-offs for shared schema, separate schemas, and separate databases.
Index, partitioning, backup, and audit logging guidance for billing operations.

output_examples.input: Move a MySQL application to PostgreSQL with minimal downtime.
output: A phased migration plan using dual writes, backfill, validation, read shadowing, and controlled cutover.
Rollback checkpoints and data consistency checks for each phase.
Operational risks for schema differences, query behavior, and replication lag.

output_examples.input: Choose storage for IoT sensor data at high write volume.
output: A comparison of time-series, columnar, and relational options.
Retention, downsampling, partitioning, and compression recommendations.
Query pattern assumptions that must be confirmed before implementation.

best_practices: Provide real access patterns, data volume estimates, latency targets, and consistency requirements before asking for architecture choices.
Ask for trade-offs and alternatives so the recommendation includes operational cost, team fit, and migration risk.
Validate any migration or schema plan in staging with backups, monitoring, and rollback criteria.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a database choice based only on a preferred vendor or framework.
Do not treat generated schemas as production ready without reviewing indexes, constraints, and data retention rules.
Do not run destructive migrations from a generated plan without human review and a tested rollback path.

faq.question: Can this skill tune slow queries?
faq.answer: It can suggest index and schema strategies, but it is mainly for architecture and data model design.

faq.question: Does it support both SQL and NoSQL systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares relational, document, key-value, wide-column, graph, time-series, search, and cloud database options.

faq.question: Can it generate diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline ERDs and Mermaid diagrams when the user asks for diagram output.

faq.question: Is it safe for production migrations?
faq.answer: It can produce a migration plan, but production execution needs human review, staging tests, backups, and rollback approval.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide domain objects, query patterns, expected scale, consistency needs, compliance rules, current stack, and team constraints.

faq.question: Can it choose managed cloud databases?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers managed database services across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud with cost and operations trade-offs.
